title: QEMU Documentation
summary: Official QEMU documentation covering the TCG dynamic translator, KVM acceleration, machine models, and device emulation framework.
facts: Hosted at qemu.org/docs
Documents TCG, KVM, Hypervisor.framework, WHPX, and HAX accelerators
Reference for QEMU's device tree, machine models, and live migration

title: Linux KVM Documentation
summary: Kernel-tree documentation for the Linux KVM hypervisor, including the ioctl API, MSR handling, nested virtualization, and architecture-specific notes.
facts: Hosted at docs.kernel.org/virt/kvm
Defines the user-space ioctl interface consumed by QEMU, crosvm, Firecracker, and Cloud Hypervisor
Includes notes on nested virtualization, SEV, SEV-ES, SEV-SNP, and TDX

title: Xen Project Documentation
summary: Xen hypervisor documentation, covering the original paravirtualization model, PVH, HVM, and the Xen security model.
facts: Hosted at wiki.xenproject.org and xenbits.xenproject.org
Originated paravirtualization on x86
Used in AWS EC2 (early instance types) and Citrix Hypervisor

title: Firecracker microVM Documentation
summary: AWS Firecracker microVM documentation. Minimal VMM written in Rust, designed for serverless workloads and used as the substrate for AWS Lambda and Fargate.
facts: Hosted at github.com/firecracker-microvm/firecracker
Built on KVM with a minimal device model (virtio-net, virtio-block, serial)
Boot times in the low tens of milliseconds; small attack surface

title: gVisor Documentation
summary: Google gVisor user-space kernel and container runtime. Implements a Linux-compatible kernel surface in user space to isolate containerized workloads.
facts: Hosted at gvisor.dev
Two platforms: ptrace and KVM
Used inside Google Cloud Run and App Engine

title: Kata Containers Documentation
summary: Kata Containers project documentation. Container runtime that launches each pod in a lightweight VM for stronger isolation while remaining OCI-compatible.
facts: Hosted at github.com/kata-containers/kata-containers
OCI-compatible runtime backed by QEMU, Cloud Hypervisor, or Firecracker
Used by industry for multi-tenant Kubernetes isolation

title: crosvm Documentation
summary: Google crosvm VMM written in Rust, the basis for Android virtualization and ChromeOS Linux apps.
facts: Hosted at chromium.googlesource.com/crosvm/crosvm
Minimal VMM built on KVM, ARM Hypervisor.framework, gunyah
Used as the substrate for Android's Virtualization Framework

title: Cloud Hypervisor Documentation
summary: Rust-based VMM (Cloud Hypervisor) focused on modern cloud workloads, supporting KVM and Microsoft Hypervisor backends.
facts: Hosted at github.com/cloud-hypervisor/cloud-hypervisor
Built on rust-vmm crate ecosystem (shared with Firecracker)
Adds virtio-fs, vhost-user devices, and live migration

title: Bochs Documentation
summary: Documentation for the Bochs x86 emulator, an interpretive full-system emulator widely used for OS bring-up, BIOS/UEFI development, and reverse engineering.
facts: Hosted at bochs.sourceforge.io
Interpretive (no TCG) x86/x86-64 emulator
Used as a reference oracle for QEMU TCG behavior

title: libvirt Documentation
summary: libvirt is the standard virtualization management API on Linux, abstracting QEMU/KVM, Xen, LXC, and bhyve under a single XML and RPC interface.
facts: Hosted at libvirt.org
Used by virt-manager, oVirt, OpenStack Nova, and KubeVirt
Defines stable domain XML schema and event API

title: MAME Documentation
summary: MAME (Multiple Arcade Machine Emulator) documentation; MAME is the canonical preservation project for arcade and legacy computer hardware.
facts: Hosted at docs.mamedev.org
Emulates thousands of arcade systems, consoles, and home computers
Used by museums and research institutions for historical hardware preservation

title: Dolphin Emulator Documentation
summary: Dolphin Wii/GameCube emulator documentation. Reference open-source implementation of PowerPC dynamic recompilation and TEV pixel pipeline.
facts: Hosted at dolphin-emu.org/docs
Implements PowerPC JIT and Flipper/Hollywood GPU emulation
Notable case study in console emulator reverse engineering

title: QEMU, a Fast and Portable Dynamic Translator
summary: Fabrice Bellard's original QEMU paper (USENIX 2005) describing the design of the QEMU dynamic translator. Foundational reference for binary translation in modern emulators.
facts: Published at USENIX 2005
Introduces the QEMU TCG basic-block translation cache
Foundation of nearly every modern open-source cross-ISA emulator

title: Linux Virtualization Documentation
summary: Aggregate Linux kernel documentation tree for virtualization features: KVM, VFIO, vhost, virtio, vDPA, and IOMMU groups.
facts: Hosted at docs.kernel.org/virt and docs.kernel.org/driver-api/vfio
Reference for IOMMU-backed device passthrough
Reference for vDPA control plane used by accelerated networking

title: Intel VT-x / VMX Overview
summary: Intel Virtualization Technology (VT-x / VMX) overview as described in the Intel SDM Volume 3C. Covers root/non-root operation, EPT, VPID, and posted interrupts.
facts: Documented in Intel SDM Vol 3C
Defines VMX root/non-root modes and the VMCS state machine
Backbone of KVM-Intel, Hyper-V, VMware, and Xen HVM

title: AMD SVM (AMD-V) Overview
summary: AMD's Secure Virtual Machine (SVM) extensions for hardware-assisted virtualization, including Nested Paging (NPT), ASID, AVIC, and SEV/SEV-ES/SEV-SNP.
facts: Documented in the AMD64 Architecture Programmer's Manual Vol 2
Defines VMCB control structure used by KVM-AMD and Xen
Includes SEV-SNP secure VM extensions
